channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New York mayor vows to regain public's trust after Justice Department orders halt to prosecution
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday thanked the Justice Department for ordering a halt to his criminal corruption case, castigating the prosecution as “cruel” while vowing to regain the trust of voters ahead of his upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Massachusetts top court rules Karen Read can be retried in her boyfriend's death
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The state’s top court ruled Tuesday that Karen Read can be retried on all the same charges in the death of her Boston police officer boyfriend, the latest twist in the long-running case that transfixed true crime fans nationwide.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Roses are red, violets are blue, 940 million flowers are traveling (through Miami) to you
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers. In the run up to Feb. 14, agricultural specialists at Miami International Airport have processed about 940 million stems of cut flowers, according to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to a fraud charge related to duping donors who gave money to a private effort to build a wall along the U.S. southern border — a case the conservative strategist has decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dogs from great Danes to terriers head into the ring at Westminster show
item.description: Big dogs are having their day at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show, where a bunch of massive breeds are being judged Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Trump won't block immigration arrests in houses of worship. Now these 27 religious groups are suing
item.description: More than two-dozen Christian and Jewish groups representing millions of Americans — ranging from the Episcopal Church and the Union for Reform Judaism to the Mennonites and Unitarian Universalists — filed a federal court lawsuit Tuesday challenging a Trump administration move giving immigration age
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canned tuna sold at Walmart, Trader Joe's, Costco, others is recalled in multiple states
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a recall on canned tuna products sold at several different retailers including Walmart, Trader Joe's, Kroger, and Costco.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tariffs rattle small business owners already dealing with tight margins
item.description: President Donald Trump's continued roll out of a wide array of tariffs is rattling small business owners already dealing with tight profit margins.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Andrew Tate, brother Tristan sued for allegedly sex trafficking American woman to foreign country
item.description: Influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been sued in Florida for allegedly sex trafficking a US woman to Romania and trying to intimidate her as a witness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The women of 'The White Lotus' check into Season 3 in Thailand
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Get ready to check into “The White Lotus” in Thailand for Season 3 — this time, sadly, there’s no room for Jennifer Coolidge, who went overboard in the second season.
item.dc:creator: HILARY FOX, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How photos lost in American disasters find their way home, with a little help from people who care
item.description: In the wake of deadly disasters, communities across the United States have banded together to search for lost photographs stuck in rivers and trees, or buried in the rubble.
item.dc:creator: Anna Furman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 4 FEMA employees fired for paying for hotels for migrants in New York City
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security told Fox News that “Four employees are being fired today for circumventing leadership and unilaterally making the egregious payment for hotels for migrants in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says who could potentially fund the rebuild of war-torn enclave and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump seeks to expand his influence over arts and culture with Kennedy Center moves
item.description: After dominating U.S. politics for nearly a decade, President Trump now appears to be setting his sights on the country’s cultural landscape.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: China expands influence near wealthy Florida enclave as migrants from communist country flood into US
item.description: Gordon G. Chang discussed China's growing influence in the Caribbean region and the national security threat it could present.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Recent aviation disasters cause fears about the safety of flying
item.description: The spate of recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington, the fiery plane crash in Philadelphia and a plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 are only the most high-profile disasters.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hegseth says Fort Bragg is coming back, but with a twist
item.description: On Monday, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth signed a memo that orders the Army to rename Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, this time in honor of a different soldier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in brutal Louisiana hammer attack on college student subject of ICE hold request
item.description: Immigration officials have filed a detained hold request on a man accused of randomly attacking a Louisiana college student last week with a hammer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Person killed in Union Station shooting during rush hour, police search for suspect
item.description: WASHINGTON (DC News Now) -- A person was shot and killed during rush hour Monday afternoon at D.C.'s Union Station. The United States Capitol Police (USCP) said officers responded to Union Station at around 4:40 p.m., where they found one person who had been shot.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gable, NewsNation

item.title: Catalytic converters stolen from Illinois district's school buses, forcing students to stay home
item.description: A suburban Illinois school district was forced to hold an emergency e-learning day after the catalytic converters were stolen from "over 80%" of its buses.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Second sinkhole opens up on I-80 in New Jersey, shutting down roadway
item.description: A 40-foot by 40-foot hole opened up at the same exit on Dec. 26, caused by the collapse of an abandoned mineshaft underneath, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Alexa Farrell, NewsNation

item.title: Mother orders son to shoot her boyfriend after argument: Police
item.description: The victim said that Prentis Lee approached his vehicle armed with a black handgun allegedly saying, "You got my momma f----- up. I will shoot you in your face.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, NewsNation

item.title: Brother of Parkland shooter arrested for trespassing at three schools
item.description: Surveillance footage recovered from Jan. 25 showed him attempting to open exterior doors and peering into the schools.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Sutherland, NewsNation

item.title: Protester speaks after being hit by car during Salt Lake City demonstration
item.description: Padilla-Oliver said the driver slowed down and stopped, after which she "stood with pride" and held her sign.
item.dc:creator: MJ Jewkes, NewsNation

item.title: Judge finds Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending
item.description: A federal judge found Monday that the Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending and told the White House to release all the money.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Third judge blocks Trump’s order ending birthright citizenship for kids of people in US illegally
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A third federal judge on Monday blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S. illegally. The ruling from U.S. District Judge Joseph N.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 6 hidden messages in Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl performance
item.description: Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime performance is growing in viewership hours after the big game, gaining more than 11 million views since it aired Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl halftime performer detained after unfurling Sudanese-Palestinian flag
item.description: A performer in Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime show was detained late Sunday after showing a Sudanese-Palestinian flag on the field.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Trump tells Treasury Department to stop making pennies
item.description: President Trump claims he has instructed the U.S. Treasury Department to stop producing pennies, calling the 1-cent coin wasteful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Is the Super Bowl halftime singer lip syncing? Check for these signs
item.description: Casual fans will be watching the theatrics, choreography and guest appearances during Kendrick Lamar and SZA's Super Bowl halftime show. Sleuths will be watching their throats.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Philadelphia radio station bans Taylor Swift music ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: A release from the station claims that 94.5 is "pressing pause on all things Taylor Swift." The announcement came after a Swift fan and a Philly advertising agency spent the week trolling each other with ads in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, ABC 4

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KRON4

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: BERKELEY, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives in programs receiving federal money has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, TERRY CHEA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Louisiana woman arrested following death of Kansas City reporter covering Super Bowl LIX
item.description: A Louisiana woman has been arrested after she was allegedly caught using the reporter's credit cards in the New Orleans area.
item.dc:creator: Raeven Poole, Fox 5
